file.c

00025 
00026 /*=========================================================================
00027  * SYSTEM:    Verifier
00028  * SUBSYSTEM: Operations on class files.
00029  * FILE:      file.c
00030  * OVERVIEW:  Routines for outputing the internal class file.
00031  *=======================================================================*/
00032 
00033 /*=========================================================================
00034  * Include files
00035  *=======================================================================*/
00036 
00037 #include "wrappers.h"
00038 
00039 #include "oobj.h"
00040 #include "jar.h"
00041 #include "tree.h"
00042 #include "sys_api.h"
00043 #include "convert_md.h"
00044 
00045 /* initialize opnames[256]; */
00046 #include <opcodes.init>
00047 
00048 /*=========================================================================
00049  * Globals and extern declarations
00050  *=======================================================================*/
00051 
00052 extern char *progname;
00053 extern bool JARfile;
00054 extern bool tmpDirExists;
00055 
00056 char *current_class_name = NULL;
00057 
00058 bool stack_map_on = true;
00059 
00060 static char *PrintableClassname(char *classname);
00061 
00062 extern char tmp_dir[32];
00063 
00064 char *output_dir = "output";
00065 unsigned char *class_buf;
00066 int class_buf_size = 0;
00067 int class_index;
00068 int last_not_utf8_index;
00069 
00070 #define INIT_CLASS_BUF_SIZE 256
00071 
00072 void ensure_capacity(int sz)
00073 {
00074     if (class_index + sz >= class_buf_size) {
00075         while (class_index + sz >= class_buf_size) {
00076             class_buf_size *= 2;
00077         }
00078     }
00079     class_buf = (unsigned char *)realloc(class_buf, class_buf_size);
00080 }
00081 
00082 void write_u1(unsigned long u1)
00083 {
00084     ensure_capacity(1);
00085     class_buf[class_index++] = (unsigned char)u1;
00086 }
00087 
00088 void write_u2(unsigned long u2)
00089 {
00090     ensure_capacity(2);
00091     class_buf[class_index++] = (unsigned char)(u2 >> 8);
00092     class_buf[class_index++] = (unsigned char)u2;
00093 }
00094 
00095 void write_u4(unsigned long u4)
00096 {
00097     ensure_capacity(4);
00098     class_buf[class_index++] = (unsigned char)(u4 >> 24);
00099     class_buf[class_index++] = (unsigned char)(u4 >> 16);
00100     class_buf[class_index++] = (unsigned char)(u4 >> 8);
00101     class_buf[class_index++] = (unsigned char)u4;
00102 }
00103 
00104 int new_utf8_index(ClassClass *cb, int index)
00105 {
00106     if (index > last_not_utf8_index) {
00107         return index + unhand(cb)->n_new_class_entries;
00108     } else {
00109         return index;
00110     }
00111 }
00112 
00113 unsigned long lookup_utf8(ClassClass *cb, char *utf8)
00114 {
00115     int i;
00116     cp_item_type *constant_pool = cbConstantPool(cb);
00117     int cpEntries, newEntries;
00118     unsigned char *type_table =
00119         constant_pool[CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_INDEX].type;
00120     cpEntries = cbConstantPoolCount(cb);
00121     newEntries = unhand(cb)->n_new_utf8_entries;
00122     for (i = 0; i < cpEntries; i++) {
00123         if (type_table[i] == (CONSTANT_Utf8 | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED) &&
00124             strcmp(utf8, constant_pool[i].cp) == 0) {
00125             return new_utf8_index(cb, i);
00126         }
00127     }
00128     for (i = 0; i < newEntries; i++) {
00129         if (strcmp(unhand(cb)->new_utf8_entries[i], utf8) == 0) {
00130             return i + cpEntries + unhand(cb)->n_new_class_entries;
00131         }
00132     }
00133     unhand(cb)->new_utf8_entries[newEntries] = utf8;
00134     unhand(cb)->n_new_utf8_entries = newEntries + 1;
00135     return newEntries + cpEntries + unhand(cb)->n_new_class_entries;
00136 }
00137 
00138 unsigned long lookup_class(ClassClass *cb, char *name)
00139 {
00140     int i;
00141     cp_item_type *constant_pool = cbConstantPool(cb);
00142     unsigned char *type_table =
00143         constant_pool[CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_INDEX].type;
00144     for (i = 0; i < cbConstantPoolCount(cb); i++) {
00145         if (type_table[i] == (CONSTANT_Class | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED) &&
00146             strcmp(name, cbName(constant_pool[i].clazz)) == 0) {
00147             return i;
00148         }
00149         if (type_table[i] == CONSTANT_Class &&
00150             strcmp(name, constant_pool[constant_pool[i].i].cp) == 0) {
00151             return i;
00152         }
00153     }
00154     panic("class expected to be in constant pool: %s", name);
00155     return 0;
00156 }
00157 
00158 int get_last_not_utf8_index(ClassClass *cb)
00159 {
00160     int i;
00161     int result;
00162     cp_item_type *constant_pool = cbConstantPool(cb);
00163     unsigned char *type_table =
00164         constant_pool[CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_INDEX].type;
00165     for (i = 0, result = -1; i < cbConstantPoolCount(cb); i++) {
00166         if (type_table[i] != (CONSTANT_Utf8 | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED)) {
00167             result = i;
00168         }
00169     }
00170     return result;
00171 }
00172 
00173 void write_constant_pool(ClassClass *cb)
00174 {
00175     int i, j;
00176     cp_item_type *constant_pool = cbConstantPool(cb);
00177     unsigned char *type_table =
00178         constant_pool[CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_INDEX].type;
00179     unsigned int sealedValue;
00180 
00181     last_not_utf8_index = get_last_not_utf8_index(cb);
00182     if (stack_map_on && unhand(cb)->has_stack_maps) {
00183         /* The maximum number of new utf8 entries we'll need is one
00184          * for "StackMap", plus one for each of the new class entries.
00185          * However, we bump the number to 2 in case we need to add another
00186          * entry for the SourceFile attribute.
00187          */
00188         unhand(cb)->n_new_utf8_entries = 0;
00189         unhand(cb)->new_utf8_entries =
00190             (char **)malloc((unhand(cb)->n_new_class_entries + 2) *
00191                             sizeof(char *));
00192         /* Put sure we have all the Utf8 entries that we need, so that
00193          * we'll know the final size of the constant pool.
00194          */
00195         lookup_utf8(cb, "StackMap");
00196         for (i = 0; i < unhand(cb)->n_new_class_entries; i++) {
00197             lookup_utf8(cb, unhand(cb)->new_class_entries[i]);
00198         }
00199     } else {
00200         unhand(cb)->n_new_utf8_entries = 0;
00201         unhand(cb)->new_utf8_entries = NULL;
00202         unhand(cb)->n_new_class_entries = 0;
00203         if (unhand(cb)->new_class_entries != NULL) {
00204             free(unhand(cb)->new_class_entries);
00205             unhand(cb)->new_class_entries = NULL;
00206         }
00207     }
00208     /* At this, we should not add have to create any more utf8 entries.
00209      * We  find the value of unhand(cb)->n_new_utf8_entries, and complain
00210      * later if this value has changed.
00211      */
00212     sealedValue = unhand(cb)->n_new_utf8_entries;
00213 
00214     write_u2(cbConstantPoolCount(cb) +
00215              unhand(cb)->n_new_class_entries +
00216              unhand(cb)->n_new_utf8_entries);
00217 
00218     for (i = 1; i < cbConstantPoolCount(cb); i++) {
00219         write_u1(type_table[i] & CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_TYPEMASK);
00220         switch(type_table[i]) {
00221         case CONSTANT_Utf8 | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED:
00222             write_u2(strlen(constant_pool[i].cp));
00223             for (j = 0; j < (int)strlen(constant_pool[i].cp); j++) {
00224                 write_u1(constant_pool[i].cp[j]);
00225             }
00226             break;
00227 
00228         case CONSTANT_Class:
00229         case CONSTANT_String:
00230             write_u2(new_utf8_index(cb, constant_pool[i].i));
00231             break;
00232 
00233         case CONSTANT_Class | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED:
00234             write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, cbName(constant_pool[i].clazz)));
00235             break;
00236 
00237         case CONSTANT_Fieldref:
00238         case CONSTANT_Methodref:
00239         case CONSTANT_InterfaceMethodref:
00240             write_u2(constant_pool[i].i >> 16);
00241             write_u2(constant_pool[i].i & 0xFFFF);
00242             break;
00243 
00244         case CONSTANT_NameAndType:
00245             write_u2(new_utf8_index(cb, constant_pool[i].i >> 16));
00246             write_u2(new_utf8_index(cb, constant_pool[i].i & 0xFFFF));
00247             break;
00248 
00249         case CONSTANT_Integer | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED:
00250         case CONSTANT_Float | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED:
00251             write_u4(constant_pool[i].i);
00252             break;
00253 
00254         case CONSTANT_Long | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED:
00255         case CONSTANT_Double | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED:
00256             write_u4(constant_pool[i].i);
00257             write_u4(constant_pool[i + 1].i);
00258             i++;
00259             break;
00260 
00261         default:
00262             panic("bad constant pool entry type: %d", type_table[i]);
00263         }
00264         if (i == last_not_utf8_index) {
00265             /* Write extra CONSTANT_Class entries created by the stackmaps */
00266             for (j = 0; j < unhand(cb)->n_new_class_entries; j++) {
00267                 char *classname = unhand(cb)->new_class_entries[j];
00268                 write_u1(CONSTANT_Class);
00269                 write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, classname));
00270             }
00271         }
00272     }
00273     /* Write extra CONSTANT_Utf8 entries created by the stackmaps */
00274     for (i = 0; i < unhand(cb)->n_new_utf8_entries; i++) {
00275         char *string = unhand(cb)->new_utf8_entries[i];
00276         int length = strlen(string);
00277         write_u1(CONSTANT_Utf8);
00278         write_u2(length);
00279         for (j = 0; j < length; j++) {
00280             write_u1(string[j]);
00281         }
00282     }
00283     if (sealedValue != (unsigned int) unhand(cb)->n_new_utf8_entries) {
00284         panic("New utf8 entries have been added???");
00285     }
00286 
00287 }
00288 
00289 void write_interfaces(ClassClass *cb)
00290 {
00291     int i;
00292     write_u2(cbImplementsCount(cb));
00293     for (i = 0; i < cbImplementsCount(cb); i++) {
00294         write_u2(cbImplements(cb)[i]);
00295     }
00296 }
00297 
00298 void write_fields(ClassClass *cb)
00299 {
00300     int i;
00301     write_u2(cbFieldsCount(cb));
00302     for (i = 0; i < cbFieldsCount(cb); i++) {
00303         struct fieldblock *fb = &cbFields(cb)[i];
00304         write_u2(fb->access & ACC_WRITTEN_FLAGS);
00305         write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, fb->name));
00306         write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, fb->signature));
00307         /* Number of attributes we're about to output.
00308          * Each item in the following sum is a boolean that returns 1 or 0.
00309          */
00310         write_u2(  ((fb->access & ACC_VALKNOWN) != 0)
00311                  + (fb->synthetic != 0)
00312                  + (fb->deprecated != 0));
00313         if (fb->access & ACC_VALKNOWN) {
00314             write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, "ConstantValue")); /* Attribute name */
00315             write_u4(2);                                /* Length */
00316             write_u2(fb->u.offset);
00317         }
00318         if (fb->deprecated) {
00319             write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, "Deprecated"));     /* Attribute name  */
00320             write_u4(0);                                 /* Length */
00321         }
00322         if (fb->synthetic) {
00323             write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, "Synthetic"));      /* Attribute name  */
00324             write_u4(0);                                 /* Length */
00325         }
00326     }
00327 }
00328 
00329 void write_stack_map(int nentries, struct map_entry *entries)
00330 {
00331     int i;
00332     for (i = 0; i < nentries; i++) {
00333         struct map_entry *entry = entries + i;
00334         write_u1(entry->type);
00335         if (entry->type == CF_ITEM_NewObject) {
00336             write_u2(entry->info);
00337         }
00338         if (entry->type == CF_ITEM_Object) {
00339             /* This is ugly
00340              * Non-negative entries refer to classes
00341              *       in the original constant pool.
00342              * Negative entries, ~(entry->info) is the entry's index
00343              *       in the list of additional constant pool entries.
00344              */
00345             if (entry->info >= 0) {
00346                 write_u2(entry->info);
00347             } else {
00348                 write_u2(last_not_utf8_index + 1 + ~entry->info);
00349             }
00350         }
00351     }
00352 }
00353 
00354 int stack_map_size(int nentries, struct map_entry *entries)
00355 {
00356     int i;
00357     int result = 0;
00358     for (i = 0; i < nentries; i++) {
00359         struct map_entry *entry = entries + i;
00360         result += 1;
00361         if (entry->type == CF_ITEM_Object || entry->type == CF_ITEM_NewObject) {
00362             result += 2;
00363         }
00364     }
00365     return result;
00366 }
00367 
00368 void write_methods(ClassClass *cb)
00369 {
00370     int i;
00371     write_u2(cbMethodsCount(cb));
00372     for (i = 0; i < cbMethodsCount(cb); i++) {
00373         struct methodblock *mb = &cbMethods(cb)[i];
00374         write_u2(mb->fb.access & ACC_WRITTEN_FLAGS);
00375         write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, mb->fb.name));
00376         write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, mb->fb.signature));
00377 
00378         /* Attribute count
00379          * Each item in the following sum is a boolean that returns 1 or 0.
00380          */
00381         write_u2(  (mb->code_length > 0)
00382                  + (mb->nexceptions > 0)
00383                  + (mb->fb.synthetic != 0)
00384                  + (mb->fb.deprecated != 0));
00385 
00386         if (mb->code_length > 0) {
00387             int j;
00388             int stack_map_attr_length = 0;   /* Stack Map attribute length */
00389             int line_no_attr_length = 0;     /* line_number_table attr Length */
00390             int localvar_attr_length = 0;    /* localVar_table attr Length */
00391             int code_attrs = 0;        /* Attributes count for Code attribute */
00392 
00393             if (stack_map_on && mb->n_stack_maps > 0) {
00394                 stack_map_attr_length = 8;
00395                 code_attrs++;                /* increment code attributes */
00396                 for (j = 0; j < mb->n_stack_maps; j++) {
00397                     stack_map_attr_length += 6 +
00398                         stack_map_size(mb->stack_maps[j].nlocals,
00399                                        mb->stack_maps[j].locals) +
00400                         stack_map_size(mb->stack_maps[j].nstacks,
00401                                        mb->stack_maps[j].stacks);
00402                 }
00403             }
00404 
00405             /* attribute_name_index for "Code" attribute */
00406             write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, "Code"));
00407             if (mb->line_number_table_length > 0) {
00408                 /* calculate the size of the line_number_table attribute */
00409                 /* Attribute length for line_number_table
00410                  * sizeof line_number_table(4) * no. of table entries + 8
00411                  * 8 bytes = 2 bytes for attr_name_index +
00412                  *           4 bytes for attr_length +
00413                  *           2 bytes for line_number_table_length
00414                  */
00415                 code_attrs++;      /* increment code attributes */
00416                 line_no_attr_length = 4 * mb->line_number_table_length + 8;
00417             }
00418             if (mb->localvar_table_length > 0) {
00419                 /* calculate the size of the localvar_table attribute */
00420                 /* Attribute length for localvar_table
00421                  * sizeof localvar_table (10) * no. of table entries + 8
00422                  * 8 bytes = 2 bytes for attr_name_index +
00423                  *           4 bytes for attr_length +
00424                  *           2 bytes for line_number_table_length
00425                  */
00426                 code_attrs++;      /* increment code attributes */
00427                 localvar_attr_length = 10 * mb->localvar_table_length + 8;
00428             }
00429 
00430             /* Attribute Length */
00431             write_u4(12 + mb->code_length
00432                      + mb->exception_table_length * 8
00433                      + stack_map_attr_length
00434                      + line_no_attr_length
00435                      + localvar_attr_length);
00436             write_u2(mb->maxstack);
00437             write_u2(mb->nlocals);
00438             write_u4(mb->code_length);
00439             for (j = 0; j < (int)mb->code_length; j++) {
00440                 write_u1(mb->code[j]);
00441             }
00442             write_u2(mb->exception_table_length);
00443             for (j = 0; j < (int)mb->exception_table_length; j++) {
00444                 write_u2(mb->exception_table[j].start_pc);
00445                 write_u2(mb->exception_table[j].end_pc);
00446                 write_u2(mb->exception_table[j].handler_pc);
00447                 write_u2(mb->exception_table[j].catchType);
00448             }
00449 
00450             /* Attributes count for Code attribute */
00451             write_u2(code_attrs);
00452 
00453             /* check if we have a valid line_number_table entries and
00454              * if so, write out the pc and line_number entries.
00455              */
00456 
00457             if (mb->line_number_table_length > 0) {
00458                 /* line_number_table attribute exists */
00459                 /* attribute_name_index for "LineNumberTable" */
00460                 write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, "LineNumberTable"));
00461                 /* Attribute length for line_number_table
00462                  * (exclude initial 6 bytes = 2 bytes for attr_name_index +
00463                  *                            4 bytes for attr_length)
00464                  */
00465                 write_u4(line_no_attr_length - 6);
00466                 /* Length of line_number_table */
00467                 write_u2(mb->line_number_table_length);
00468                 /* write out the line_number_table entries */
00469                 for (j=0; j< (int) mb->line_number_table_length; j++) {
00470                     if (mb->line_number_table != NULL) {
00471                         write_u2(mb->line_number_table[j].pc);
00472                         write_u2(mb->line_number_table[j].line_number);
00473                     }
00474                 }
00475             }
00476 
00477             /* check if we have a valid localvar_table entries and
00478              * if so, write out its entries
00479              */
00480 
00481             if (mb->localvar_table_length > 0) {
00482                 /* localvar_table attribute exists */
00483                 /* attribute_name_index for "LocalVariableTable" */
00484                 write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, "LocalVariableTable"));
00485 
00486                 /* Attribute length for localvar_table
00487                  * (exclude initial 6 bytes = 2 bytes for attr_name_index +
00488                  *                            4 bytes for attr_length)
00489                  */
00490                 write_u4(localvar_attr_length - 6);
00491 
00492                 /* Length of localvar_table */
00493                 write_u2(mb->localvar_table_length);
00494 
00495                 /* write out the localvar_table entries */
00496                 for (j=0; j< (int) mb->localvar_table_length; j++) {
00497                     if (mb->localvar_table != NULL) {
00498                         write_u2(mb->localvar_table[j].pc0);
00499                         write_u2(mb->localvar_table[j].length);
00500                         write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb,mb->localvar_table[j].name));
00501                         write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb,mb->localvar_table[j].signature));
00502                         write_u2(mb->localvar_table[j].slot);
00503                     }
00504                 }
00505             }
00506 
00507             if (stack_map_on && mb->n_stack_maps > 0) {
00508                 write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, "StackMap"));
00509                 write_u4(stack_map_attr_length - 6);
00510                 write_u2(mb->n_stack_maps);
00511                 for (j = 0; j < mb->n_stack_maps; j++) {
00512                     write_u2(mb->stack_maps[j].offset);
00513                     write_u2(mb->stack_maps[j].nlocals);
00514                     write_stack_map(mb->stack_maps[j].nlocals,
00515                                     mb->stack_maps[j].locals);
00516                     write_u2(mb->stack_maps[j].nstacks);
00517                     write_stack_map(mb->stack_maps[j].nstacks,
00518                                     mb->stack_maps[j].stacks);
00519                 }
00520             }
00521         }
00522         if (mb->nexceptions > 0) {
00523             int j;
00524             write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, "Exceptions"));
00525             write_u4(2 + mb->nexceptions * 2);
00526             write_u2(mb->nexceptions);
00527             for (j = 0; j < mb->nexceptions; j++) {
00528                 write_u2(mb->exceptions[j]);
00529             }
00530         }
00531         if (mb->fb.deprecated) {
00532             write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, "Deprecated"));
00533             write_u4(0);                                 /* Length */
00534         }
00535         if (mb->fb.synthetic) {
00536             write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, "Synthetic"));
00537             write_u4(0);                                 /* Length */
00538         }
00539     }
00540 }
00541 
00542 void ensure_dir_exists(char *dir)
00543 {
00544     struct stat stat_buf;
00545     char *parent;
00546     char *q;
00547     if (dir[0] == 0) {
00548         return;
00549     }
00550     parent = strdup(dir);
00551     q = strrchr(parent, '/');
00552     if (q) {
00553         *q = 0;
00554         ensure_dir_exists(parent);
00555     }
00556     if (stat(dir, &stat_buf) < 0) {
00557         if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose) {
00558             jio_fprintf(stderr, "Creating output directory [%s]\n", dir);
00559         }
00560         //mkdir(dir, 0755);
00561         #if HAVE_MKDIR_ONE_ARGUMENT
00562         mkdir(dir);
00563         #else
00564         mkdir(dir, 0755);
00565         #endif
00566     }
00567     free(parent);
00568 }
00569 
00570 void ensure_dir_writable(char *dir)
00571 {
00572     struct stat stat_buf;
00573 
00574     stat(dir, &stat_buf);
00575     if ((stat_buf.st_mode & S_IFMT) == S_IFDIR) { /* is dir ? */
00576         if (access(dir, R_OK | W_OK) < 0) {
00577             panic("%s is write protected\n", dir);
00578         }
00579     } else {
00580         panic("%s is not a directory\n", dir);
00581     }
00582 }
00583 
00584 void
00585 WriteClass(ClassClass *cb)
00586 {
00587     int fd;
00588     char fname[1024];
00589     char buff[BUFSIZ];
00590     char *nativeName = &buff[0];
00591 
00592     class_buf = (unsigned char*)malloc(INIT_CLASS_BUF_SIZE);
00593     if (class_buf == NULL) {
00594         panic("out of memory");
00595     }
00596     class_buf_size = INIT_CLASS_BUF_SIZE;
00597 
00598     class_index = 0;
00599 
00600     write_u4(0xcafebabe);
00601 
00602     write_u2(cbMinorVersion(cb));
00603     write_u2(cbMajorVersion(cb));
00604 
00605     write_constant_pool(cb);
00606 
00607     write_u2(cbAccess(cb) & ACC_WRITTEN_FLAGS);
00608 
00609     write_u2(lookup_class(cb, cbName(cb)));
00610     write_u2(cbSuperclass(cb)
00611              ? lookup_class(cb,  cbName(cbSuperclass(cb))) : 0);
00612     write_interfaces(cb);
00613     write_fields(cb);
00614     write_methods(cb);
00615 
00616     /* Output number of attributes
00617      * Each item in the following sum is a boolean that returns 1 or 0.
00618      */
00619     write_u2(   (cbSourceName(cb) != NULL)
00620               + (cbAbsoluteSourceName(cb) != NULL)
00621               + (unhand(cb)->hasTimeStamp)
00622               + (unhand(cb)->deprecated)
00623               + (unhand(cb)->synthetic)
00624               + (cbInnerClasses(cb) != NULL)
00625             );
00626     if (cbSourceName(cb) != NULL) {
00627         /* write the source file attribute used for debugging purposes */
00628         write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, "SourceFile"));     /* SourceFile attribute */
00629         write_u4(2);                                 /* Length */
00630                                  /* CP entry containing the source name */
00631         write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, cbSourceName(cb)));
00632     }
00633     if (cbAbsoluteSourceName(cb) != NULL) {
00634         /* write the source file attribute used for debugging purposes */
00635         write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, "AbsoluteSourcePath"));
00636         write_u4(2);
00637         write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, cbAbsoluteSourceName(cb)));
00638     }
00639     if (unhand(cb)->hasTimeStamp) {
00640         write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, "TimeStamp"));
00641         write_u4(8);
00642         write_u4(cbTimestamp(cb).high);
00643         write_u4(cbTimestamp(cb).low);
00644     }
00645     if (unhand(cb)->deprecated) {
00646         write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, "Deprecated"));     /* attribute name */
00647         write_u4(0);                                 /* Length */
00648     }
00649     if (unhand(cb)->synthetic) {
00650         write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, "Synthetic"));     /* attribute name */
00651         write_u4(0);                                /* Length */
00652     }
00653 
00654     if (cbInnerClasses(cb) != NULL) {
00655         int count = cbInnerClassesCount(cb);
00656         struct innerClasses *thisInnerClass= cbInnerClasses(cb);
00657         struct innerClasses *lastInnerClass = thisInnerClass + count;
00658         write_u2(lookup_utf8(cb, "InnerClasses"));  /* Attribute name */
00659         write_u4(8 * count + 2);                    /* Length */
00660         write_u2(count);
00661         for ( ; thisInnerClass < lastInnerClass; thisInnerClass++) {
00662             char *innerName = thisInnerClass->inner_name;
00663             write_u2(thisInnerClass->inner_class);
00664             write_u2(thisInnerClass->outer_class);
00665             write_u2( (innerName != 0) ? lookup_utf8(cb, innerName) : 0);
00666             write_u2(thisInnerClass->access);
00667         }
00668     }
00669 
00670     /* Conversion for Japanese filenames */
00671     utf2native(cbName(cb), nativeName, BUFSIZ);
00672 
00673     if (JARfile) {
00674         /* classes need to be put in a JAR file */
00675         sprintf(fname, "%s/%s.class", tmp_dir, nativeName);
00676     } else {
00677         sprintf(fname, "%s/%s.class", output_dir, nativeName);
00678     }
00679 
00680     {
00681         char *dir = strdup(fname);
00682         char *q;
00683 
00684         q = strrchr(dir, '/');
00685         if (q) {
00686             *q = 0;
00687             ensure_dir_exists(dir);
00688             ensure_dir_writable(dir);
00689         }
00690         free(dir);
00691     }
00692 
00693     fd = open(fname, O_WRONLY | O_CREAT | O_TRUNC , 0644);
00694 
00695     if (fd < 0) {
00696         panic("failed to open %s", fname);
00697     }
00698 
00699     tmpDirExists = true;   /* tmpDir exists with verified classes */
00700     write(fd, class_buf, class_index);
00701     close(fd);
00702     free(class_buf);
00703     class_buf_size = 0;
00704 }
00705 
00706 void
00707 VerifyFile(char *fn)
00708 {
00709     /* If this is the first class, we'll run into problems if loading the
00710      * class forces Object to be loaded, when then forces this class to
00711      * be loaded.  To prohibit such problems, we force Object to be loaded
00712      */
00713     FindClass(0, "java/lang/Object", true);
00714 
00715     {
00716         ClassClass *cb = FindClass(0, fn, true);
00717         char *class_name = PrintableClassname(fn);
00718 
00719         if (cb == NULL) {
00720             errorCode = 1;  /* set error status to indicate error */
00721             jio_fprintf(stderr, "Error loading class %s\n", class_name);
00722         } else {
00723             if (no_native_methods) {
00724                 /* Check for native methods in classes */
00725                 struct methodblock *mb;
00726                 int size;
00727                 mb = cbMethods(cb);
00728                 for (size=0; size < (int) cbMethodsCount(cb); size++, mb++) {
00729                     if (mb->fb.access & ACC_NATIVE) {
00730                         current_class_name = fn;
00731                         panic("native methods should not appear");
00732                     }
00733                 }
00734             }
00735             WriteClass(cb);
00736         }
00737     }
00738 }
00739 
00740 char *PrintableClassname(char *class_name)
00741 {
00742     char *p;
00743     static char class_copy[257];
00744     strncpy(class_copy, class_name, 256);
00745 
00746     /* Convert all slashes in the classname to periods */
00747     for (p = class_copy; ((p = strchr(p, '/')) != 0); *p++ = '.');
00748     return class_copy;
00749 }
00750 
00751 void printCurrentClassName(void)
00752 {
00753     if (current_class_name) {
00754         fprintf(stderr, "Error preverifying class %s\n    ",
00755                 PrintableClassname(current_class_name));
00756     }
00757 }
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:28 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
